title: AI Lab Watch
summary: Interactive tracker scoring frontier AI labs on safety practices. Browse for 15 minutes to get a sense of where labs sit.

title: How to turn your AI evil
summary: Interactive walkthrough showing how easily models can be steered toward harmful behaviour. Hands-on intro to alignment failures.

title: BlueDot — Technical AI Safety course
summary: Structured part-time course in technical AI safety. The standard recommendation for people moving from technical backgrounds into the field.

title: Introduction to Mechanistic Interpretability
summary: Friendly written intro to what mech interp is and why it matters — start here before deeper technical reading.

title: The Misguided Quest for Mechanistic AI Interpretability
summary: Counterargument piece pushing back on the case for mech interp as a safety strategy. Worth reading after the standard intros to sharpen your view.

title: On the Biology of a Large Language Model
summary: Detailed mechanistic-interpretability paper studying how features and circuits inside a model give rise to behaviour.

title: Tracing the thoughts of a large language model
summary: Accessible writeup of interpretability research showing how researchers can follow a model's internal reasoning.

title: Transformer Circuits — Anthropic interpretability research
summary: Anthropic's mechanistic-interpretability research site. Browse for the deepest current work on how models work internally.

title: Introduction to AI Control
summary: Friendly intro to the AI control research agenda — what 'control' means and how it differs from alignment.

title: When AI Chooses Harm Over Failure
summary: Interactive demo of agentic misalignment — try it to see how an AI agent can prefer a harmful action over admitting failure.

title: Redwood Research reading list
summary: Curated reading list from Redwood Research covering AI control and scalable oversight.

title: AI Control with Buck Shlegeris and Ryan Greenblatt
summary: Long-form conversation with two researchers leading work on AI control — what it means, why it matters, where it's going.

title: Plausible AI takeover scenarios
summary: Ryan Greenblatt walks through concrete scenarios for how an AI takeover could play out — useful for grounding abstract risk arguments.

title: AI Control: Improving Safety Despite Intentional Subversion
summary: Original paper introducing the AI control research agenda — the framing of evaluating safety even under adversarial model behaviour.

title: Redwood Research
summary: Research org leading work on AI control and adversarial robustness.

title: Auditing language models for hidden objectives
summary: Article on auditing models for objectives they've internalised but don't reveal — sits between mech interp and scheming work.

title: Secret languages of AI
summary: Interactive demo on how AI systems can communicate in ways humans don't notice — a tactile way into the deception risk.

title: Frontier Models are Capable of In-context Scheming
summary: Empirical paper showing frontier models can engage in scheming behaviour given the right context. The reference paper for current concerns.

title: AI's Capacity for Deception
summary: Accessible article covering recent results on AI deception — good intro before the technical paper.

title: Marius Hobbhahn on the race to solve AI scheming before models go superhuman
summary: Apollo Research's CEO on what AI scheming actually looks like, why it's hard to detect, and what concrete defences exist today.

title: Apollo Research
summary: Research org focused on evaluations of frontier-model deception and scheming.

title: Technical AI safety upskilling resources
summary: Curated guide to upskilling for technical AI safety — courses, papers, and programmes for someone moving in from a technical background.

title: Career review: AI safety researcher
summary: What the AI-safety-research career path actually looks like — what people do day to day, how to get in, where to start.

title: Career review: AI hardware expert
summary: Why AI hardware expertise matters for safety, and how to build it. Useful for engineers considering a more technical, less-crowded angle on the field.

title: Eval project ideas
summary: Working list of evaluation project ideas — pick one that fits your skills and try it. A great starting point for a hackathon-shaped technical contribution.

title: 45 mech interp project ideas (Apollo Research)
summary: List of 45 concrete mechanistic-interpretability project ideas — pick one and start. Most are scoped small enough to attempt in a weekend.
